rationale: The citing paper uses Castao et al. (2008) in its conceptual development section as direct empirical support for a key mechanism: temporal distance shifts decision weight from cost/feasibility concerns to performance/desirability concerns. That mechanism is then used to justify the paper's prediction that larger booking lead times should increase preference for higher-quality hotels. The citation is not just a background list item; it helps ground the logic for one of the focal circumstantial variables.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Castao et al. (2008) as prior evidence that temporal distance changes attribute weighting in choice. It uses that insight as conceptual grounding for its own quality-versus-price predictions in the hotel reservation context.
